key_insight: The paper verifies a sequential, non-iterative thermo-poroelastic coupling strategy in PFLOTRAN that solves flow/heat first and mechanics second while still matching benchmark solutions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a validated THM modeling foundation for subsurface energy systems such as enhanced geothermal reservoirs and thermal energy storage in porous media.
method: Implicitly solve pressure and temperature on control-volume/Voronoi cells.
Update quasi-static mechanics on an element-based dual mesh using fixed-stress splitting.
Map flow and mechanical degrees of freedom to handle discontinuities such as fractures.
evidence_signal: The implementation is verified against analytical and semi-analytical poroelastic and thermo-poroelastic benchmarks for pressure diffusion, temperature evolution, and deformation.
watch_item: Because the coupling is strictly sequential and non-iterative, accuracy and stability may be more sensitive in strongly coupled or highly discontinuous cases.
paper_title: Verification of a sequential thermo-poroelasticity formulation in PFLOTRAN
